_meta.description: Stated Qatar government priorities, KPIs, and acknowledged challenges for transportation/infrastructure. Collected 2026-05-12.
_meta.limitations: Qatar's full NDS3 PDF (gco.gov.qa) returned 403 to fetcher; relying on official news summary + Oxford Business Group + Ministry of Transport press releases.

documents.name: Qatar National Vision 2030 (QNV2030)
transport_relevant_signals: Environmental Development pillar: harmony between growth and environment
Implementation supported by Transport Master Plan 2050 (TMPQ 2050)

documents.name: Third Qatar National Development Strategy (NDS3) 2024-2030
transport_priorities: Three growth clusters: logistics, manufacturing, tourism
Establish Qatar as re-export centre for high-value products
Strengthen air transport sector for international competitiveness
kpis: 25% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions by 2030 (cross-sector, transport is a contributor)

documents.name: Ministry of Transport Strategy 2025-2030 ('Reaching Beyond Horizons')
stated_priorities: World-class infrastructure for logistics performance and digital transformation
Enhance public transportation efficiency (reliable, convenient, eco-friendly)
Smart mobility innovation and advanced technologies integration
Improve traffic safety standards and reduce carbon emissions
Human capital development and local workforce upskilling
kpis: 73% public bus electrification achieved (2024 Q1) -> 100% by 2030
35% of all vehicles electric by 2030 (per QNECCS, cross-referenced)
Double transportation industry's contribution to GDP
Qatar ranks #1 in region for electric transit switching
documents.vision_quote: integrated, secure, resilient, and sustainable transport ecosystem

documents.name: Qatar Freight Master Plan (QFMP)
stated_priorities: Top 15 globally in Logistics Performance Index (target)
Reduce truck-movement impact on traffic flow
Automation, data-driven logistics, smart freight systems

documents.name: TASMU Smart Qatar - Transportation Pillar
kpis: 10% reduction in average vehicle trip emissions
documents.implication: Many high-priority TASMU use cases remain 'not initiated' — opportunity for research that quantifies the demand or impact of these.

documents.name: Qatar Public Transport Master Plan (QPTMP) - in progress
documents.stated_intent: Study the status quo of traffic congestion of both private vehicles and public transport, and carbon emissions; propose solutions.
documents.signal: Government is ACTIVELY surveying public to inform this plan as of June 2025 — clear signal that congestion + modal split data are policy-relevant right now.

documents.name: Qatar Autonomous Vehicle Strategy 2025-2030
stated_priorities: Driverless vehicle trials (electric taxis already piloted)

explicit_acknowledgements: Government's QPTMP survey (June 2025) explicitly intends to STUDY status quo of congestion — implies they lack systematic baseline data
Doha Metro <30% adoption among economically active population (per QU/MDPI 2025) — public data finding cited in funded research
Truck-traffic interaction with passenger flow flagged by QFMP
Many TASMU smart-mobility use cases unfunded after 6+ years — signals execution gap
implicit_signals: Despite >$100B spent on roads (95% of transport investment), congestion remains a stated concern in multiple strategy documents
Vehicle fleet grew 13.7% YoY in 2024 (62,163 new registrations Jan-Aug 2024) — supply-demand pressure unmodeled
Three-peak daily traffic pattern (06:30/12:30/17:15) is unique to Qatar but minimally studied
Government has set sharp 2030 KPIs (35% EV fleet, 100% electric buses, 25% GHG cut) but no published academic evaluation of plausibility / interim progress
